channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Pregnant migrant girls are being sent to a Texas shelter flagged as medically risky
item.description: Government officials and advocates for the children worry the goal is to concentrate them in Texas, where abortion is banned.
item.content:encoded:
Government officials and advocates for the children worry the goal is to concentrate them in Texas, where abortion is banned.

(Image credit: Patricia Lim)
item.dc:creator: Mark Betancourt

item.title: The 2026 World Cup faces big challenges with only 100 days to go
item.description: Will Iran compete? Will violence in Mexico flare up? And what about funding for host cities in the U.S.? With only 100 days left before it begins, the 2026 World Cup is facing a lot of uncertainty.
item.content:encoded:
Will Iran compete? Will violence in Mexico flare up? And what about funding for host cities in the U.S.? With only 100 days left before it begins, the 2026 World Cup is facing a lot of uncertainty.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: President Trump is trying to make it harder to vote. Here's why that matters
item.description: Trump is promoting tighter restrictions on mail-in ballots as well as passage of the SAVE Act, which requires proof of citizenship to vote. UCLA professor Richard Hasen unpacks the ramifications.
item.content:encoded: Trump is promoting tighter restrictions on mail-in ballots as well as passage of the SAVE Act, which requires proof of citizenship to vote. UCLA professor Richard Hasen unpacks the ramifications.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: College students, professors are making their own AI rules. They don't always agree
item.description: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.content:encoded: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: Senators pressed Kristi Noem on the DHS shutdown and shootings. Here are key moments
item.description: Secretary of Homeland Security Kristi Noem testified amid a pause in funding to her agency and increased bipartisan scrutiny of her leadership.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of Homeland Security Kristi Noem testified amid a pause in funding to her agency and increased bipartisan scrutiny of her leadership.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Trump promised the MAGA base no new wars. Then he went to war with Iran
item.description: President Trump promised his "Make America Great Again" voters an "America First" foreign policy. With the war in Iran, he's testing MAGA world's willingness to be flexible on one of its core beliefs.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump promised his "Make America Great Again" voters an "America First" foreign policy. With the war in Iran, he's testing MAGA world's willingness to be flexible on one of its core beliefs.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Trump wants to reach 1 million apprenticeships. Arkansas is jumpstarting the effort
item.description: The Trump administration has a goal of reaching 1 million registered apprenticeships. It's put Arkansas in charge of leading the way.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has a goal of reaching 1 million registered apprenticeships. It's put Arkansas in charge of leading the way.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Freedom Plane flies historical docs across the U.S. for 250th birthday celebration
item.description: The National Archives Freedom Plane will make Kansas City its first stop on its national tour. The plane will carry some of America's most significant founding-era documents. The tour is in honor of America's 250th anniversary.
item.content:encoded: The National Archives Freedom Plane will make Kansas City its first stop on its national tour. The plane will carry some of America's most significant founding-era documents. The tour is in honor of America's 250th anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: Investigators still searching for answers after deadly Austin bar shooting
item.description: Authorities in Austin, Texas, are still searching for answers two days after a shooting at a bar left four dead, including the suspected shooter, and more than a dozen people injured.
item.content:encoded: Authorities in Austin, Texas, are still searching for answers two days after a shooting at a bar left four dead, including the suspected shooter, and more than a dozen people injured.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Weber

item.title: In Vermont, small town meetings grapple with debate on big issues
item.description: Typically concerned with local issues, residents at town meetings in Vermont and elsewhere increasingly use the forum to debate polarizing national and international events.
item.content:encoded:
Typically concerned with local issues, residents at town meetings in Vermont and elsewhere increasingly use the forum to debate polarizing national and international events.

(Image credit: JOSEPH PREZIOSO)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith
